scope.summary: Implement smooth corner geometry for topstitch paths by inserting arc-approximating segments at path vertices where the interior angle is less than 150 degrees, with a configurable bevel radius in mm
acceptance_criteria: smooth_topstitch_corners(path: &TopstitchPath, bevel_radius_mm: f32) -> TopstitchPath inserts arc segments at path vertices with interior_angle_deg < 150.0
Bevel radius is stored per topstitch path in the GarmentSpec internal guide line definition alongside the existing path coordinates
The arc approximation uses 3-segment cubic Bezier evaluation (4 points per corner) to stay within 0.1mm of a true arc at the default bevel radius of 2.0mm
Smooth corners are visible in the viewport solid render as rounded topstitch junctions
Smooth corner path is also reflected in the texture-baked topstitch line drawn by MT-226
proof_targets: HBR-VIS: capture a shirt collar with topstitch path; assert corner joints show curved bevels not sharp angles in the solid render
scope.risk_if_missed: Without smooth corners, topstitch paths on garments (pocket edges, collar lines, waistband) show sharp 90-degree joints that are visually wrong. Realistic topstitch rendering is a baseline quality signal for fashion-production accuracy and directly affects the Fap Test for garment render quality.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
